mnemonics: Swing is LIGHT because it's built on AWT's HEAVY foundations. Think: 'Swing is Lightweight, AWT is Heavyweight'.
keyPoints: Swing components are lightweight, meaning they are rendered using pure Java code and are not dependent on native peer components.
Swing offers a much richer set of GUI components compared to AWT (e.g., tables, trees, styled text panes).
Swing follows the Model-View-Controller (MVC) architecture, which separates the data, visual representation, and control logic of a component.
commonMistakes: Mixing AWT and Swing components haphazardly, especially using AWT heavyweight components (like Canvas) on top of Swing lightweight ones, which can cause rendering issues.
Performing long-running tasks on the Event Dispatch Thread (EDT), which will freeze the GUI. These tasks should be offloaded to a worker thread.
Not using SwingUtilities.invokeLater() to update the GUI from threads other than the EDT, which can lead to thread-safety issues and erratic behavior.
